meta.coverage: 42 intents: Top resident questions + full RVA 311 service catalog coverage
meta.fallback_message: I’m not sure about that one.

sample_questions: I got a tax bill I can't pay
Can't afford my real estate taxes
What happens if I don't pay my property tax
I'm behind on taxes what do I do
Tax bill too high need help
owe the city money for taxes
got a letter about unpaid property taxes
scared im gonna lose my house over taxes
how to set up a tax payment plan
how much are my real estate taxes
look up my tax bill
where to pay my taxes
questions.answer: Look up your Richmond real estate tax bill and pay online at rva.gov/online-payments. The tax rate is (assessed value / $100) x $1.20. Due dates: 1st half January 14, 2nd half June 14. If you can't pay by the due date, you can set up a payment arrangement through the Delinquent Collections Unit — you can do this even BEFORE your taxes are past due. Contact RVA 311 at 804-646-7000 to set one up. Important: you can only have one payment plan for one tax type at a time. If you're 65+ or permanently disabled, you may qualify for the OAPD Tax Relief Program. Don't ignore the bill — consequences include state tax refund seizure and inability to register vehicles at DMV.
action_steps: Look up and pay your bill at rva.gov/online-payments
Or pay at City Hall, 900 E. Broad Street
If you can't pay: call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000 for a payment arrangement
If 65+ or disabled: ask about the OAPD Tax Relief Program
Have your tax bill or account number ready
questions.deadlines: Real estate tax: 1st half due January 14, 2nd half due June 14
questions.handoff_trigger: If the person mentions they've already received a collections notice from an outside agency, tell them they must work with that agency directly, not the city.

sample_questions: I'm a senior can I get tax relief
Tax relief for elderly
Disabled tax exemption Richmond
OAPD program
My mom is 70 and can't pay property tax
im 70 years old and cant afford my taxes
tax break for disabled homeowners
do veterans get tax relief in richmond
tax freeze program how do I apply
questions.answer: Richmond has the OAPD (Older Adults and Persons with Disabilities) Real Estate Tax Relief Program. If you're 65+ or permanently/totally disabled, you may qualify for partial or full exemption from real estate taxes on your primary residence. There's also a Tax Freeze option (recently expanded by City Council for 2025+) that freezes your assessment to the prior year's value. Even if you were denied before, the expanded Freeze option may now cover you. Veterans with 100% service-connected disability are fully exempt with no income requirement.
action_steps: Get the OAPD application from rva.gov/finance/oapd-relief
Submit application by September 30 for timely processing (legal deadline is December 31)
Bring: proof of income, bank/investment statements, proof of disability (if under 65)
Apply at City Hall Real Estate Unit: 900 E. Broad St, Room 100
For recertification in years 2-3, return the form by December 31
questions.deadlines: Legal filing deadline: December 31 annually. Submit by September 30 for timely processing on your next tax bill.

sources.title: RVA 311 - Tax Relief for Older Adults/Disabled

questions.handoff_trigger: If they have delinquent taxes AND qualify for OAPD, there are special exceptions to the one-plan-at-a-time rule. Route to 311.

sample_questions: My tax bill is wrong
Property assessment too high
How to appeal real estate tax
My house is not worth that much
Tax bill seems incorrect
my property assessment is way too high
how to dispute my tax bill
they overvalued my house
questions.answer: If you think your real estate tax bill is wrong, there are two different issues to check. First: is the ASSESSMENT (property value) wrong? You can appeal through the Real Estate Assessor's Office. Use the Richmond Parcel Mapper to check your current assessed value. The tax is calculated as: (assessed value / $100) × $1.20 rate. Second: is there a BILLING error? The city has had known issues with incorrect bills. For billing discrepancies, contact RVA 311.
action_steps: Check your assessed value at the Richmond Parcel Mapper (Assessor's website)
For assessment appeals: contact the Real Estate Assessor's Office
For billing errors: call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000
Keep your bill and any supporting documents (comparable sales, etc.)
questions.deadlines: Appeals must be filed within the timeframe specified on your assessment notice
questions.handoff_trigger: Always route to 311 for billing discrepancies. The system should not attempt to resolve specific account issues.

sample_questions: How do I pay my car tax
Personal property tax on vehicle
Car tax bill Richmond
PPTRA what is it
Can I get my car tax reduced
how much is car tax in richmond
vehicle tax bill when is it due
my car value is wrong on the tax bill
do I get relief on my car tax
questions.answer: Personal property tax in Richmond covers motor vehicles, boats, trailers, and manufactured homes. You may qualify for Personal Property Tax Relief (PPTRA): vehicles with assessed value of $1,000 or less get full exemption; vehicles over $1,000 get partial relief on the first $20,000 of value. The vehicle must be for personal use (not >50% business). If your car's value seems too high, you can request a reassessment based on high mileage or poor condition using forms on the Finance Assistance page. Veterans with 100% service-connected disability can get full motor vehicle exemption.
action_steps: Pay online at the Finance Department Online Payment Portal
For reassessment: download the High Mileage or Condition form from rva.gov/finance/assistance
Submit reassessment with mileage/condition as of January 1 (the tax date)
For disabled veteran exemption: submit VA letter + certification form
questions.deadlines: Personal property tax due date varies — check your bill

sources.title: RVA 311 - Vehicle Personal Property Tax Inquiry

sample_questions: Can't pay my water bill
Help with utility bills
Utility assistance programs Richmond
My gas bill is too high
Going to get disconnected need help
about to get my water shut off
what is metrocare program
help paying electric bill
lights about to get cut off need help
where do I pay my water bill
pay my utility bill online
how to pay my water bill
questions.answer: Pay your City of Richmond utility bill (water, gas, wastewater) online at apps.richmondgov.com/applications/DPUAccountLookUp or by calling DPU at 804-646-4646. If you need help paying: MetroCare Assistance Program provides up to $1,500/year for water, wastewater, and gas bills if your household income is at or below 225% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines. PromisePay Payment Plans offer flexible options for customers who can't pay in full. Call 804-646-4646 for either program. LIHEAP (heating assistance): call Social Services at 804-646-7046. If service is already disconnected: pay your bill, then call 804-646-4646 for same-day reconnection ($35 extra fee).
action_steps: Pay online at apps.richmondgov.com/applications/DPUAccountLookUp
Or call DPU at 804-646-4646 to pay by phone
For help paying: ask about MetroCare and PromisePay at 804-646-4646
For heating assistance (LIHEAP): call Social Services at 804-646-7046
If disconnected: pay bill, then call 804-646-4646 for reconnection
questions.deadlines: Apply for MetroCare anytime (subject to funding). PromisePay plans: new plans after Sept 1 2025 limited to 12 months.
questions.handoff_trigger: If service is already disconnected and they need same-day reconnection, route directly to 804-646-4646.

sample_questions: Is the water safe to drink
Is there a boil water advisory
Water crisis update
Something wrong with my water
Water smells funny
my water looks brown
tap water tastes weird
is my water contaminated
water smells like chlorine is it safe
questions.answer: For the most current water safety status, check rva.gov or call 311 at 804-646-7000. The city's water treatment plant has implemented new standard operating procedures since the January 2025 crisis. If you notice discolored water, unusual smell, or low pressure, report it immediately through RVA 311. For emergencies, call DPU at 804-646-4646.
action_steps: Check rva.gov for current advisories
Sign up for Richmond Ready Alerts for emergency notifications
If water looks/smells wrong: call DPU at 804-646-4646 immediately
For general updates: call 311 at 804-646-7000
questions.handoff_trigger: ALWAYS check for active advisories before responding. This answer must be dynamically updated. In an emergency, push alerts proactively.

sample_questions: Water bill too high
Utility bill doesn't look right

my water bill is way higher than normal
being overcharged for water
DPU charged me wrong
how to dispute my utility bill
questions.answer: Richmond has had known issues with incorrect utility bills. If your bill seems wrong: First, look up your account using the DPU Account Look-Up Tool at apps.richmondgov.com. You'll need your street number, street name, and last 4 of your SSN. Then call DPU at 804-646-4646 to dispute. You can also check if your meter has been misread. The city has been working to improve billing accuracy as part of the Mayoral Action Plan.
action_steps: Look up your account at apps.richmondgov.com/applications/DPUAccountLookUp
Call DPU Customer Service at 804-646-4646
Have your account number and recent bills ready
Ask about the Equal Monthly Payment Plan to smooth out seasonal variation
questions.handoff_trigger: Route to DPU for all billing disputes.

sample_questions: How do I get food stamps
Apply for SNAP
Need help buying food
EBT card application
I'm hungry and need help
where do I apply for food stamps in richmond
lost my job need food help
how to get an EBT card
cant afford groceries for my family
food stamp office near me
questions.answer: You can apply for SNAP (food stamps) several ways. Fastest: apply online at commonhelp.virginia.gov. You can also call 855-635-4370, or fax a paper application to 804-646-7018, or drop it off at the Southside Plaza office (4100 Hull Street). You'll need an interview with a benefit program specialist. Bring: photo ID, proof of residence, proof of income, and housing cost info. Income limits for FY2026: $2,888/month gross for a household of 3 (130% FPL). Virginia uses broad-based categorical eligibility at 200% FPL, which raises the gross income limit to approximately $4,440/month for a household of 3. Benefits are loaded onto an EBT card monthly. You can also use SNAP at farmers markets — check the Virginia Fresh Match program for half-price produce.
action_steps: Apply online at commonhelp.virginia.gov (fastest)
Or drop off paper application at 4100 Hull Street Road
Gather: photo ID, proof of address, income proof, housing costs
Wait for interview scheduling — keep your phone available
questions.deadlines: Apply anytime. Benefits are prorated from the date you apply, so apply ASAP.
questions.handoff_trigger: If the person says they have no food right now, also provide: Central Virginia Food Bank 804-521-2500 and FeedMore food pantry locator.

sample_questions: How to get Medicaid
Need health insurance can't afford it
Medical coverage for my kids
FAMIS application
No health insurance what do I do
free health insurance for kids
how to apply for medicaid in virginia
lost my job no health insurance
coverva how does it work
questions.answer: Apply for Medicaid online at commonhelp.virginia.gov or through Virginia's Insurance Marketplace at marketplace.virginia.gov. You can also call 855-635-4370. If you apply at the Marketplace and are found eligible for Medicaid or FAMIS (children's coverage), you'll be automatically enrolled. Children may qualify for FAMIS even if parents don't qualify for Medicaid. Paper applications can be dropped off at Richmond Social Services offices: Downtown at 300 E. Franklin Street, or Southside at 4100 Hull Street Road.
action_steps: Apply at commonhelp.virginia.gov or marketplace.virginia.gov
Or visit Social Services at 300 E. Franklin St (Downtown) or 4100 Hull St (Southside)
Have: SSNs for everyone applying, income proof, current insurance info if any
questions.deadlines: Apply anytime. Coverage can be retroactive to beginning of the month you apply.
questions.handoff_trigger: If they need immediate medical care and are uninsured, provide: Richmond Health District 804-482-5525 and RVA Health free/low-cost clinics.

sample_questions: I need help paying rent
About to be evicted
Rental assistance programs
Can't afford my apartment
Emergency housing help
facing eviction need help now
behind on rent 2 months
nowhere to go about to be homeless
landlord is kicking me out what do I do
questions.answer: For rental assistance in Richmond, start with these: Virginia 211 (dial 2-1-1) connects you to local emergency rental assistance 24/7. Contact Richmond Social Services through RVA 311 at 804-646-7000. For after-hours Adult Protective Services emergencies: 888-832-3858. Also check: RRHA (Richmond Redevelopment and Housing Authority) for Section 8/Housing Choice Voucher waitlist status. The Virginia Rent Relief Program website (if still active) or CommonHelp may have additional options. Community organizations like United Way, Salvation Army, and local churches may also provide one-time emergency help.
action_steps: Call 2-1-1 (Virginia 211) for immediate referrals
Contact Richmond Social Services via RVA 311 at 804-646-7000
After hours Adult Protective Services: 888-832-3858
Check commonhelp.virginia.gov for benefit programs
If facing eviction: contact Virginia Legal Aid at 866-534-5243
questions.deadlines: If you have an eviction notice, act immediately — call 211 first.
questions.handoff_trigger: If imminent eviction (court date set), route to Virginia Legal Aid and 211 immediately.

sample_questions: Check my benefits status
When will I get my SNAP
Application still pending
How long does it take to get approved

I applied for snap 3 weeks ago no answer

questions.answer: Check the status of any benefits application (SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, Energy Assistance) at commonhelp.virginia.gov — log into your account to see updates. You can also call the Enterprise Customer Service Center at 855-635-4370. Processing times vary: SNAP applications must be processed within 30 days (7 days for expedited/emergency cases). Medicaid: typically 45 days. If you haven't heard back within these windows, call to follow up.
action_steps: Log in at commonhelp.virginia.gov to check status
Have your case number or SSN ready
If past the processing deadline, ask to speak to a supervisor
questions.deadlines: SNAP: 30 days (7 days expedited). Medicaid: 45 days.

sample_questions: Where do I go to apply for help

questions.answer: Richmond Social Services has two main offices. Downtown: 300 E. Franklin Street, Richmond VA 23219. South Richmond: Southside Community Services Center, 4100 Hull Street Road, Richmond VA 23224 (has a drop box for applications). You can also skip the line by applying online at commonhelp.virginia.gov. For general city services: City Hall is at 900 E. Broad Street. For 311 requests: call 804-646-7000 or visit rva311.com.
action_steps: Downtown office: 300 E. Franklin Street
Southside office: 4100 Hull Street Road (has drop box)
City Hall: 900 E. Broad Street
Or skip the line: commonhelp.virginia.gov

sample_questions: Help with heating bill
LIHEAP application

no heat in my house and its cold

winter heating help programs
questions.answer: LIHEAP (Low Income Heating Energy Assistance) helps with heating costs. Apply through CommonHelp online or call Richmond Social Services at 804-646-7046. Other options: Dominion Energy's EnergyShare program, Salvation Army, Catholic Charities, United Way, and local churches. For DPU gas customers: the MetroCare program provides up to $1,500/year — call 804-646-4646.
action_steps: Apply at commonhelp.virginia.gov
Or call Social Services at 804-646-7046
For DPU gas bills: call 804-646-4646 for MetroCare
Also try Dominion Energy EnergyShare: 866-366-4357
questions.deadlines: LIHEAP typically available October through March (heating season), subject to funding.
questions.handoff_trigger: If the person has no heat and it's winter, this is urgent. Provide 211 and Social Services emergency number 888-832-3858.

sample_questions: How to report a pothole
huge pothole on my block
pothole on my road needs patching
road has a big pothole near my house
who do I call about a pothole
pothole damaged my car
there is a dangerous pothole on my street
report pothole in richmond
pothole on my road needs fixing
questions.answer: Report potholes on city streets through RVA 311. You can: call 804-646-7000, use the RVA311 app (iOS/Android), or submit online at rva311.com. Potholes on city streets are patched with asphalt within 14 days, weather permitting. Important: for potholes in ALLEYS, use the alley repair request instead (different form). For sinkholes (road caving in, large collapse), call DPU at 804-646-4646 press 1 for 12-hour emergency response — sinkholes are handled by Public Utilities, not Public Works.
action_steps: Submit a pothole report at rva311.com
For alley potholes: use the alley repair form at rva311.com instead
Include: exact location, description, photo if possible
questions.deadlines: Potholes are patched within 14 days, weather permitting.
questions.handoff_trigger: For emergencies that pose immediate danger to persons or property, call 804-646-5100 (non-emergency police).

sample_questions: When is trash pickup
Missed trash collection
Recycling schedule
Bulk pickup request
My trash wasn't picked up
they didnt pick up my trash this week
how do I get rid of a couch
when does recycling get picked up
need a new trash can
questions.answer: For your specific trash and recycling pickup schedule, check rva.gov and enter your address, or call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000. If your trash was missed, submit a report through RVA 311 — they'll schedule a makeup pickup. For bulk/large item pickup, you can request free pickup (limited items) or paid pickup for larger loads through 311. Need a new trash can? Request one through rva311.com. Recycling is collected on the same day of the week as trash, but every other week (biweekly).
action_steps: Check your schedule at rva.gov (search 'trash collection')
For missed pickup: report at rva311.com
For bulk items: request free or paid pickup through 311
Request a new trash can at rva311.com
Put carts at the curb by 7 AM on collection day

sample_questions: How to get a business license
Start a business in Richmond
BPOL tax
Small business license cost
Home business permit
I want to open a shop in richmond
how much does a business license cost
do I need a permit for a home business
renew my business license
questions.answer: To operate a business in Richmond, you need a Business, Professional, and Occupational License (BPOL). Apply through the RVA Business Portal online. You'll need your business information, EIN or SSN, and estimated gross receipts. The Department of Finance handles business licenses. You may also need zoning approval depending on your business type and location. For help navigating the process, call RVA 311 or the Department of Finance.
action_steps: Apply at the RVA Business Portal (online)
Or visit Finance Department at 900 E. Broad Street
Have ready: business name, type, address, EIN, estimated revenue
Check zoning requirements for your location
Call 311 if unsure which permits you need
questions.deadlines: Must obtain license before operating. Annual renewal required.

sources.title: RVA 311 - Business Personal Property and Manufacturing & Tools

questions.handoff_trigger: If they're asking about permits for construction/renovation, route to Development Review.

sample_questions: Where can I use a computer for free
Free internet access Richmond
Library with WiFi near me
Need to print something
Don't have internet at home

questions.answer: Richmond Public Libraries offer free computer and internet access. Multiple branches across the city have computers available for public use and free WiFi. The city's Community Resources page lists libraries as a primary access point. Main Library: 101 E. Franklin Street. For hours and locations of all branches, check rvalibrary.org or call 804-646-7223. Libraries also offer free faxing, free scanning, and technology help.
action_steps: Find your nearest library at rvalibrary.org
Or call 804-646-7223 for hours and locations
Bring a photo ID for computer use
WiFi is free — no library card needed for WiFi

sample_questions: How to pay a parking ticket

Contest a parking ticket

questions.answer: Pay parking tickets online through the City's Online Payment page at rva.gov/online-payments (uses Remit Online portal). You can also pay at City Hall (900 E. Broad Street) or by mail. If you want to contest a ticket, check the back of the citation for instructions or contact RVA 311. Note: speed camera violations (school zone cameras) use a separate portal at cite-web.com, also linked from the Online Payments page.
action_steps: Pay online at rva.gov/online-payments
Or pay at City Hall, 900 E. Broad Street
To contest: follow instructions on the back of the ticket
For delinquent notices: submit inquiry at rva311.com
Have your citation number ready
questions.deadlines: Pay within the timeframe on your ticket to avoid additional penalties.

sample_questions: How do I get emergency alerts
Sign up for water alerts
Richmond Ready alerts
Emergency notification system
How will I know about emergencies
how to sign up for city alerts
text alerts for water emergencies
richmond emergency notification signup
severe storm information richmond
questions.answer: Sign up for Richmond Ready Alerts to get emergency notifications via text, email, or phone. You can sign up at rva.gov (search 'emergency alerts'). This is how the city communicates about water advisories, severe weather, and other emergencies. You should also follow @CityRichmondVA on social media. For storm-related issues like downed trees or non-functioning traffic signals, call non-emergency police at 804-646-5100. For real-time updates during an active emergency, call 311 (though during the water crisis, 311 info was sometimes delayed — so check multiple sources).
action_steps: Sign up for Richmond Ready Alerts at rva.gov
Follow @CityRichmondVA on social media
Save 311 number (804-646-7000) in your phone
During emergencies: check rva.gov and local news
For storm damage (downed trees, signals): call 804-646-5100

sources.title: RVA 311 - How to Request Police Attendance at Community Event

questions.handoff_trigger: If there's an active emergency, switch to push-notification mode.

sample_questions: Help paying for childcare
Child care assistance program
Can't afford daycare
Childcare subsidy Richmond
Need help with child care so I can work
need help paying for daycare so I can work
free preschool programs richmond
child care voucher virginia
head start program near me
questions.answer: Virginia's Child Care Subsidy Program helps eligible families pay for childcare so they can work or attend education programs. Apply through CommonHelp at commonhelp.virginia.gov or call 855-635-4370. Richmond Social Services administers the program locally. Eligibility is based on income, family size, and whether you're working or in school. The program also covers after-school care. For Head Start (free pre-K for low-income families), contact the local Head Start program.
action_steps: Apply at commonhelp.virginia.gov
Or visit Social Services at 4100 Hull Street Road or 300 E. Franklin St
Have: proof of employment/school enrollment, income, childcare provider info
questions.deadlines: Apply anytime. Benefits start from date of application.

questions.answer: Richmond Animal Care and Control handles stray animals, loose animals, animal cruelty reports, and dangerous animal complaints. Call 804-646-5573 to report any of these issues. The facility is located at 1600 Chamberlayne Avenue, Richmond VA 23222. Hours: Wednesday-Friday 12-6 PM, Saturday 12-5 PM, Sunday 12-4 PM, Monday-Tuesday by appointment only. For dead animal removal on public property, call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000.
action_steps: Call Animal Care and Control at 804-646-5573
Or submit a request at rva311.com
Visit the shelter at 1600 Chamberlayne Avenue, Richmond VA 23222
Hours: Wed-Fri 12-6 PM, Sat 12-5 PM, Sun 12-4 PM, Mon-Tue by appointment
Provide the exact location and description of the animal when reporting

sources.title: RVA 311 - Contact Animal Care and Control

questions.handoff_trigger: If the person reports an animal that is actively attacking or posing immediate danger, advise them to call 911 first.

questions.answer: Jury duty in Richmond takes place at the John Marshall Courts Building, 400 North 9th Street, Richmond VA 23219. Hours are 8:30 AM-4:30 PM, Monday-Friday. Jury duty pays $50 per day, and the term is one day per week for one month. Call the jury duty line at 804-646-0802 the night before your scheduled date to confirm whether you need to report. For general jury questions, call 804-646-6501.
action_steps: Call the jury duty line at 804-646-0802 the night before to confirm reporting
For questions about your summons, call 804-646-6501
Report to John Marshall Courts Building, 400 North 9th Street, Richmond VA 23219
Bring your jury summons and a photo ID
questions.deadlines: Call 804-646-0802 the night before each scheduled jury date to confirm whether you must report.

sources.title: Jury | Office of the Circuit Court Clerk | Richmond

questions.handoff_trigger: If the person needs to request a postponement or exemption, direct them to call 804-646-6501.

sample_questions: how to get a marriage license in richmond
how much is a marriage license

courthouse wedding richmond
marriage license appointment

marriage license requirements virginia
where to get married in richmond
questions.answer: Marriage licenses in Richmond are issued at the John Marshall Courts Building, 400 North 9th Street, Richmond VA 23219. The fee is $30 (cash or card accepted, no checks). Both parties must appear in person with a valid photo ID. An appointment is required — call 804-646-6767 to schedule. Office hours are Monday-Friday 8:30 AM-4:15 PM. The license is valid for 60 days once issued. Both parties must be 18 or older. No blood test is required.
action_steps: Call 804-646-6767 to schedule a required appointment
Both parties must appear in person with photo ID
Bring $30 (cash or card, no checks)
Go to John Marshall Courts Building, 400 North 9th Street, Richmond VA 23219
Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30 AM-4:15 PM
questions.deadlines: The marriage license is valid for 60 days after issuance.

sources.title: Marriages & Licenses | Office of the Circuit Court Clerk | Richmond

sample_questions: do I need a building permit

questions.answer: Building permits, zoning, and inspections in Richmond are handled by the Department of Planning and Development Review. An online permit portal is available for applications. For help navigating the permit process, contact RVA 311 at 804-646-7000. Forms are available at rva.gov/planning-development-review/forms. If you need to appeal a zoning decision, the Board of Zoning Appeals handles those cases.
action_steps: Visit rva.gov/planning-development-review for the online permit portal
Download forms at rva.gov/planning-development-review/forms
Call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000 for help navigating the permit process
For zoning appeals, contact the Board of Zoning Appeals through the same department
questions.handoff_trigger: If the person is unsure whether their project needs a permit, route them to RVA 311 at 804-646-7000 for guidance.

sample_questions: how to register to vote in richmond
voter registration deadline
can I register to vote online
where do I vote in richmond
am I registered to vote
change my voter registration address
register to vote virginia
when is the deadline to register
questions.answer: You can register to vote online at elections.virginia.gov/citizen-portal/. You must be a US citizen, a Virginia resident, and 18 or older to vote (17-year-olds can pre-register). You will need your Social Security Number to register. The registration deadline is 11 days before each election. You can also register at the DMV, public libraries, and government offices. For questions, call the toll-free voter line at 800-552-9745.
action_steps: Register online at elections.virginia.gov/citizen-portal/
Or register in person at the DMV, libraries, or government offices
Have your Social Security Number ready
For questions, call 800-552-9745
questions.deadlines: Voter registration deadline is 11 days before each election.

sources.title: How to Register | Virginia Department of Elections

questions.answer: Richmond Public Schools enrollment is handled through the online portal at enrollrps.schoolmint.com. You can also call 804-780-7710 or email enrollrps@rvaschools.net for assistance. The enrollment office is at 301 North Ninth Street, Richmond VA. You will need two proofs of residency to enroll. Free preschool programs are available for children turning 3 or 4 by September 30 through Head Start and the Virginia Preschool Initiative (VPI).
action_steps: Enroll online at enrollrps.schoolmint.com
Or call 804-780-7710 for enrollment help
Or email enrollrps@rvaschools.net
Gather two proofs of residency before applying
For free preschool (ages 3-4), ask about Head Start and VPI programs
questions.handoff_trigger: If the family is newly arrived or experiencing homelessness, they may be eligible for immediate enrollment under the McKinney-Vento Act. Route to 804-780-7710.

sample_questions: how to get a birth certificate in richmond

questions.answer: Birth and death certificates in Richmond are available through the Virginia Department of Health, Richmond City Health District. The fee is $12 per certificate. The office is located at Room 126, 400 East Cary Street, Richmond VA 23219. Phone: 804-205-3911. Hours: Monday-Friday 8 AM-4 PM. You will need a completed application and a photo ID. You can also apply online at vr-fhs.vdh.virginia.gov, or by mail or fax.
action_steps: Visit the office at Room 126, 400 East Cary Street, Richmond VA 23219
Or apply online at vr-fhs.vdh.virginia.gov
Bring a completed application and photo ID
Fee is $12 per certificate
Call 804-205-3911 for questions

questions.answer: Richmond's Office of Aging and Disability Services provides help for adults 55+ and persons with disabilities 18+. Services include home visits, care coordination, Medicaid applications, fuel assistance, and SNAP help. Call the senior help line at 804-646-1082 or email oads@rva.gov. The office is at 7945 Forest Hill Avenue, Building 26, Richmond VA 23225. For Meals on Wheels and meal delivery, contact FeedMore at 804-521-5200.

Or email oads@rva.gov
Visit the office at 7945 Forest Hill Avenue, Building 26, Richmond VA 23225
For Meals on Wheels: call FeedMore at 804-521-5200
Services available for adults 55+ and persons with disabilities 18+

sample_questions: my car got towed where is it
towed vehicle lookup richmond

abandoned car on my block

tow lot richmond virginia
how to get my car back after towing
questions.answer: If your car was towed in Richmond, use the tow lookup tool at apps.richmondgov.com/applications/towviewing to find it. The tool shows the vehicle's make, model, color, VIN, and plate number. Records are available for 14 days. Check the tow website before reporting your car stolen. To report an abandoned vehicle on a city street, submit a request through rva311.com. For further help, call the non-emergency police line at 804-646-5100 or RVA 311 at 804-646-7000.
action_steps: Search the tow lookup tool at apps.richmondgov.com/applications/towviewing
Records are available for 14 days after towing
To report an abandoned vehicle: submit at rva311.com
If your vehicle is not listed, call non-emergency police at 804-646-5100
Check the tow website before reporting your car as stolen
questions.deadlines: Tow records are available for 14 days. Act quickly to avoid additional storage fees.

sources.title: RVA 311 - Report Abandoned Vehicle on City Street

questions.handoff_trigger: If the person believes their car was towed illegally or from private property, advise them to contact the non-emergency police line at 804-646-5100.

sample_questions: free legal help in richmond
legal aid for eviction
tenant rights virginia
my landlord won't fix things
free lawyer for low income
being evicted need legal help
landlord tenant dispute richmond
how to fight an eviction in court
questions.answer: Free civil legal help is available through Virginia Legal Aid. Call the Legal Aid Hotline at 866-534-5243. For seniors, call the Senior Legal Helpline at 844-802-5910. Services cover evictions, foreclosure, landlord-tenant rights, consumer issues, family law, and debt. The website at valegalaid.org also has self-help tools with DIY forms for common legal issues. This is free civil legal information and assistance for those who qualify.
action_steps: Call the Legal Aid Hotline at 866-534-5243
For seniors: call the Senior Legal Helpline at 844-802-5910
Visit valegalaid.org for self-help tools and DIY legal forms
Have your documents ready: lease, notices, correspondence with landlord, court papers
questions.handoff_trigger: If the person has a court date coming up soon, urge them to call 866-534-5243 immediately as legal aid may be able to provide same-day assistance for eviction cases.

sample_questions: the sidewalk on my street is broken
how do I report a damaged sidewalk
alley behind my house needs repair
road is falling apart on my block
curb is crumbling in front of my house
there's a sinkhole on my street
bridge near me needs repair
pavement markings faded on my road
who fixes sidewalks in richmond
questions.answer: Report road, sidewalk, alley, curb, bridge, or sinkhole damage through RVA 311. The Department of Public Works handles these repairs. For sinkholes, call 804-646-4646 and press 1 for a response within 12 hours — sinkholes are often caused by underground infrastructure issues. Sidewalk repairs may take up to 365 days. Road and alley repairs may take up to 180 days. Curb and gutter repairs may take up to 180 days. For emergencies posing immediate danger, call 804-646-5100.
action_steps: Submit a request at rva311.com for your specific issue
For sinkholes: call 804-646-4646 and press 1 (24-hour, response within 12 hours)
For emergencies: call 804-646-5100
Include: exact address or intersection, description, photo if possible
Track your request status at rva311.com
questions.deadlines: Sidewalks: up to 365 days. Roads/alleys/curbs: up to 180 days. Sinkholes: 12-hour response.
questions.handoff_trigger: If reporting a sinkhole, stress urgency: call 804-646-4646 and press 1 immediately. Sinkholes can indicate major infrastructure failure.

questions.answer: Report traffic signal, stop sign, and street sign problems through RVA 311. For stop signs down or traffic signals completely out, this is a safety emergency — call non-emergency police at 804-646-5100 immediately. For non-emergency signal repairs (e.g., a light that's slow to change), submit a request at rva311.com. For neighborhood roadway safety concerns like speeding or dangerous intersections, you can also submit a request through 311.
action_steps: For stop sign down or signal out: call 804-646-5100 immediately
For non-emergency signal repair: submit at rva311.com
For street sign repair/replacement: submit at rva311.com
For speeding/safety concerns: submit at rva311.com
Include: exact intersection, which sign/signal, what's wrong
questions.deadlines: Stop sign/signal emergencies: immediate response. Non-emergency repairs: varies.
questions.handoff_trigger: If a stop sign is down or traffic signal is completely out at a busy intersection, this is a safety emergency. Direct to 804-646-5100 immediately.

sample_questions: streetlight on my block is out

request streetlight for safety
light pole is damaged on my street
questions.answer: Request a new streetlight or report a broken one through RVA 311. Submit your request at rva311.com or call 804-646-7000. For a broken streetlight, include the pole number (usually on a metal tag on the pole), the exact address, and whether the light is completely out, flickering, or stays on during the day. For gas streetlamps (not electric), call DPU at 804-646-4646 option 1 — gas lamps are handled by the Department of Public Utilities, not Public Works. For a new streetlight, describe the location and safety concern.
action_steps: Submit a streetlight repair request at rva311.com
Or request a new streetlight at rva311.com
Or call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000
Include: pole number (if visible), exact address, description of problem

questions.answer: The City of Richmond handles trees and vegetation on public property and rights of way. Request a new tree planting for free through RVA 311 — the city plants trees in eligible public spaces. For existing tree issues (dead trees, overgrown branches, stump removal), submit an assessment request through rva311.com. For vegetation blocking signs, signals, or sight lines at intersections, the city will maintain it. Note: trees on private property are the owner's responsibility.
action_steps: Request a new tree: submit at rva311.com
For tree trimming, removal, or stump: submit assessment request at rva311.com
For vegetation blocking road/sidewalk visibility: submit at rva311.com
For fallen trees blocking roads during storms: call 804-646-5100
Include: exact address, type of issue, photo if possible

questions.answer: Report park and trail issues through RVA 311. This includes damaged equipment, broken benches or fences, flooded or muddy trails, fallen trees on trails, and general park maintenance concerns. For community garden issues (neglected plots, compost problems, pest issues), submit a separate request through 311. Richmond Parks and Recreation manages these facilities.

sample_questions: standing water won’t drain on my street

questions.answer: Report stormwater and drainage issues through RVA 311. This includes standing water, clogged storm drains, flooding after rain, missing manhole covers, and illicit discharge into stormwater. The Department of Public Utilities handles stormwater infrastructure. For missing manhole covers or water leaks from utility infrastructure, call 804-646-4646 and press 1 for 24-hour response — these can be safety hazards. For standing water or clogged drains, submit a request at rva311.com.
action_steps: For missing manhole cover or water leak: call 804-646-4646 and press 1 (24-hour)
For clogged drain or standing water: submit at rva311.com
For illicit discharge (dumping into drain): submit at rva311.com
Or call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000
Include: exact location, how long the issue has persisted, photos if available
questions.deadlines: Missing manhole covers: emergency response. Drain cleaning/repair: varies.
questions.handoff_trigger: If reporting a missing manhole cover, this is a safety emergency. Direct to 804-646-4646 press 1 immediately.

sample_questions: sewer backup in my house
sewage coming up in my yard

bad smell coming from the sewer

sewer line is backed up
questions.answer: Report sewer problems to the City of Richmond through RVA 311. For sewer backups, overflows, or missing manhole covers, call 804-646-4646 and press 1 for 24-hour emergency response. You can also submit a request online at rva311.com. Sewer issues including backups, overflows, odors, and manhole problems are handled by the Department of Public Utilities. For emergencies (sewage flooding into your home or overflowing onto the street), call 804-646-4646 immediately — do not wait to submit online.
action_steps: For emergencies (backup/overflow): call 804-646-4646 and press 1 immediately
For sewer odor or manhole issues: submit at rva311.com
Or call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000
Include: exact address, description, whether sewage is inside your home
Do not attempt to open or enter sewer manholes yourself
questions.deadlines: Sewer emergencies: 24-hour response via 804-646-4646.
questions.handoff_trigger: If sewage is flooding into their home, this is an emergency. Direct them to call 804-646-4646 and press 1 immediately.

questions.answer: Report property code violations through RVA 311. Code enforcement covers: property maintenance issues (peeling paint, broken stairs, overgrown yards), unlawful accumulation of trash or debris, unsafe building/structural conditions, zoning violations, and construction without a permit. These reports go to the Department of Planning and Development Review for inspection. All reports are confidential — your information will not be shared with the property owner.
action_steps: Submit a property maintenance report at rva311.com
For unsafe structures: submit at rva311.com
For illegal construction: submit at rva311.com
Or call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000
Include: property address, description of violation, photos if possible
questions.handoff_trigger: If reporting a building that poses immediate danger (about to collapse, exposed wiring, gas leak), call 804-646-5100 for emergency response.

questions.answer: Report illegal dumping, litter on public property, and graffiti through RVA 311. Illegal dumping includes trash, debris, tires, or toxic materials dumped on public or vacant property. Graffiti on public and private property can also be reported. For litter accumulated on public sidewalks, streets, or rights of way, submit a cleanup request. These reports go to the appropriate city department for investigation and cleanup.

questions.answer: Report pest and vermin issues through RVA 311. For rats, mice, or roaches coming from the city sewer system, the Department of Public Utilities handles treatment. For infestations on private property (apartments, houses), code enforcement may be involved. For mosquito breeding on public property (standing water, stagnant pools), the Richmond City Health District can investigate. For dead animal removal on public roads and property, call RVA 311. Private property pest control is the property owner's responsibility.
action_steps: For sewer vermin (rats, roaches): submit at rva311.com
For private property infestation: submit at rva311.com (code enforcement)
For mosquitoes on public property: submit at rva311.com
For dead animal removal: call RVA 311 at 804-646-7000
Include: exact location, type of pest, severity of problem

questions.answer: For residential parking permits, abandoned vehicles, and parking violations in Richmond, use RVA 311. Residential parking permits are available for designated neighborhoods — submit a request through rva311.com. To report an abandoned vehicle on a city street (no tags, hasn't moved in 10+ days), submit through 311 or call the non-emergency police line at 804-646-5100. For parking violations (fire hydrant, crosswalk, blocking driveway), call 804-646-5100. For scooter issues (blocking sidewalks, abandoned), report through 311.
action_steps: For parking permits: submit at rva311.com

For abandoned vehicles on private property: submit code enforcement at rva311.com

questions.answer: Report neighborhood speeding and traffic safety concerns through RVA 311. Important: if someone is speeding RIGHT NOW, call non-emergency police at 804-646-5100 — do not submit a 311 request for active incidents. For ongoing speeding problems (cars always speed on your street, dangerous intersection near a school), submit a request at rva311.com for speed enforcement — this goes to Richmond Police for targeted patrol. For gas leaks, call 911 immediately if you smell gas strongly, or call DPU at 804-646-4646 for city gas concerns.
action_steps: For speeding enforcement: submit at rva311.com
For gas leak emergency: call 911 immediately
For DPU gas concerns: call 804-646-4646
For intersection safety concerns: submit at rva311.com
Include: street name, time of day speeding occurs, any details
